sessions.title: Welcome & Kickoff
sessions.set_the_stage:
Welcome. Look around this room. Every person sitting here is a Minifounder. Over this weekend and the two weeks that follow, you will build a real micro-venture and show it to your parents on Demo Day.

This is not a coding class. It is not a school project. You are building a small real business with AI as your builder. A working web tool. A website. A pitch deck. A demo recording. All yours.

Thirty minutes to set the shape, then we start.

questions.title: What is a micro-venture?
questions.body: A tiny real business that solves one problem for one kind of customer. Someone runs a fruit orchard and rents trees online. Someone runs a small tiffin service. The orchard owner did not need a million customers. They needed twenty. That is a micro-venture.

questions.title: What will it feel like on Demo Day?
questions.body: Two weeks from now, parents and friends in the room. You stand up. Three minutes. You walk them through what you built and who it is for. Write down one line: On Demo Day I will show that I _______. Keep it visible.

build.title: What you walk out with
build.instructions: Seven artifacts on one portfolio page. Ideation. AI-built app. Website. Audio. Video. Brochure. Pitch deck. Plus your three-minute Demo Day recording.

build.title: The shape of the program
build.instructions: Two days here. Then Week 1 you build a pitch deck with online office hours. Then Week 2 is in-person Demo Day with your parents. Total four weeks from first idea to standing on stage.

build.title: How the room works
build.instructions: If you are stuck for more than ten minutes, raise your hand. Asking for help is how everyone in this room finishes.

sessions.title: Pick your idea
sessions.set_the_stage:
Sixty minutes to land on the one micro-venture you will build over the next two weeks.

Five ideas are on the screen. Each one is a real micro-venture that someone you know could actually run. You pick one. Then you make it yours.

By the end of this session you have one sheet of paper. Your Ideation sheet. WHO you are building for, WHAT their problem is, and WHAT your First Build will do for them. The Ideation sheet is the first artifact on your portfolio.

questions.title: Which of the five ideas pulls you in?
questions.body: Read all five on the screen. Notice which one you keep coming back to. That is usually the right one. Trust the pull.

questions.title: Whose face do you see when you read it?
questions.body: If you picked Rent-a-Plant, whose mother or aunty grows the plants? Yours? Your friend's? Write the name. The face makes the idea real.

hmv.instruction: Is the person who would actually run this venture someone you know? Can you call them today and ask one question?
Is the problem something they actually face? Or something you imagined? When in doubt, message them now.
Can you show this person what you built in two weeks? If they live too far or you cannot speak to them, pick a different person.

prompts.title: Sharpen your idea with Perplexity
prompts.content: I am a Class 9 student building a small web app for [PERSON YOU PICKED, e.g. 'my mother who grows plants on our balcony']. The app will [WHAT YOUR APP DOES, e.g. 'show a catalog of plants people can rent for three months and book them online']. Help me understand: what are 3 other people who already do something like this in India? What did they get right? What is the one thing my version should do differently because I am building it small and local? Keep your answer short and practical.

build.title: Read the 5 idea cards on the screen
build.instructions: Five real micro-ventures on the next screen. Read each one carefully. Each card tells you the customer, the problem, what the app does, and what makes it real.

build.title: Pick one and personalise
build.instructions: Pick the one that pulls you in. Then make it specific to your life. The Rent-a-Plant idea is a starting point. It becomes different when you pick your own mother's balcony plants, your own neighbour aunty's nursery, your own friend's terrace garden.

build.title: Write the Ideation sheet
build.instructions: One A4 sheet. Top: WHO (the name of the person who will run this venture). Below: WHAT THEY STRUGGLE WITH (one sentence). Below: WHAT MY FIRST BUILD WILL DO (three lines, plain English).

build.title: Share with the room
build.instructions: Last ten minutes, every Minifounder reads their sheet aloud. Two sentences. Who you are building for, what you are building. The room listens.

output_capture.label: Ideation sheet
output_capture.instruction: Photograph your Ideation sheet. Upload to your portfolio folder. Artifact 1 of 7.

sessions.title: Day 1 lunch
sessions.set_the_stage:
Fifty minutes. Eat. Talk to the Minifounder sitting next to you about their idea. Sometimes the best feedback comes between bites.

When you come back, you build.

build.title: The lunch round
build.instructions: Eat. Stretch. Talk to one person you have not spoken to yet. Find out what they are building and for whom. Be back at 12:30 sharp.

sessions.title: Build your app — part 1
sessions.set_the_stage:
Two hours. You build the working app at the heart of your First Build.

The app is one HTML file. Not many files. One. You open Claude.ai and tell it what your app does. Claude writes the HTML. You copy the HTML, open it in your browser, see it work, then ask Claude to change things. Each time Claude rewrites the whole file. You replace the old file. Refresh the browser. Look again.

Do not write code yourself. You direct, Claude builds. By the end of this block your app does the main thing it is supposed to do. Not perfectly. Usefully.

prompts.title: Starter prompt for Claude.ai
prompts.content:
I am a Class 9 student building a simple working web app. It is for a real micro-venture, not a fake AI demo. Here is the venture:

[PASTE YOUR IDEATION SHEET]

Build me ONE complete HTML file (with CSS and JavaScript inside) that does this. It should NOT call any external AI or API. All data should be inside the HTML file itself (a JavaScript array of objects is fine). The app should have:

1. A simple landing screen explaining what this is
2. A main screen showing the catalog / list / options
3. A form where the customer makes their choice
4. A confirmation screen showing what they chose and what happens next

Use soft warm colours. Mobile-first layout. No external libraries unless absolutely needed. Give me the full file in one block so I can copy-paste it.

build.title: Open Claude.ai in one tab
build.instructions: Claude.ai is the only tool we use for app building this weekend. Do NOT use it for anything else (we have other tools for those). One tab open. One conversation.

build.title: Paste the starter prompt with your Ideation sheet filled in
build.instructions: Use the prompt above. Paste in your real Ideation sheet where the brackets are. Hit send. Claude will return one big HTML file.

build.title: Save the file as firstbuild-app.html
build.instructions: Copy the entire HTML code from Claude. Open a text editor (Notepad on Windows, TextEdit on Mac). Paste. Save as firstbuild-app.html. Double-click the file. It opens in your browser. That is your app.

build.title: Now make it yours
build.instructions: Tell Claude what to change. 'Add three more options to the catalog.' 'Change the colour to green.' 'Show a thank-you message after the form.' Each time, Claude rewrites the whole file. You replace, refresh, look. Keep going until it works for your specific person.

test_output.title: Test with the person sitting next to you
test_output.instruction: Open your app on their laptop or phone. Do not explain. Let them figure it out. Watch their face. If they look confused, the app needs a fix. If they smile, you are doing well.

sessions.title: Build your app — part 2 + deploy
sessions.set_the_stage:
Two hours and fifteen minutes. Part 2 of the build, then deploy.

For the first ninety minutes, keep iterating with Claude.ai. Add the small details that make the app feel real. Better wording. Better catalog content. A nice confirmation page. The app should feel like something a customer would actually use, not a school project.

The last forty-five minutes, you put the app on the internet. Netlify drag-and-drop. Three minutes. Then a real URL. Then your mother can open it on her phone.

build.title: Keep iterating with Claude.ai
build.instructions: Add details that make the app feel real. More items in the catalog. Better wording. Nicer confirmation message. Aim for the app to be something the actual customer would smile at.

build.title: Pause and review at 16:00
build.instructions: Stop iterating at 16:00 sharp. The next forty-five minutes are about deploying. Do not get pulled into more polish.

build.title: Deploy to Netlify
build.instructions: Go to netlify.com/drop. Drag your firstbuild-app.html file onto the page. Within three minutes you get a URL. Open the URL on your phone. Send the URL to the person sitting next to you.

build.title: End of Day 1
build.instructions: Save your Netlify URL. Save your HTML file. Bring both tomorrow. See you at 10:00.

output_capture.label: Your app URL
output_capture.instruction: Save the Netlify URL. Artifact 2 of 7.

sessions.title: Design your website
sessions.set_the_stage:
Day 2. We start with the website.

The app is the engine. The website is the shopfront. A stranger arrives at your website knowing nothing. In thirty seconds they should know what this is, who it is for, and what to do next.

First hour, we DESIGN the website. We do not build it yet. We figure out what goes on it, in what order, with what words. We use ChatGPT for the website copy this morning, NOT Claude.ai (we are saving Claude for app polish later if needed).

prompts.title: Website copy prompt for ChatGPT
prompts.content:
I am a Class 9 student. I built a small web app for [PERSON]. The app does [WHAT IT DOES]. Now I am writing the words for a one-page website that will introduce my app to visitors.

Give me the words for a one-page website with these three sections:

1. HEADLINE: One line at the top. Should make a visitor understand what this is in 5 seconds. Plain English. No business jargon.
2. WHO IS THIS FOR: Two short lines. Name the person or kind of person who will use this.
3. HOW IT WORKS: Three numbered steps. Each step one short sentence.

Write it the way an Indian school student would speak, not the way a corporate brochure would. No long words. No fluff.

build.title: Open ChatGPT in a new tab
build.instructions: Use ChatGPT for this. Not Claude.ai. We are spreading work across tools so no single account runs out.

build.title: Paste the prompt with your details
build.instructions: Fill in PERSON and WHAT IT DOES with your real venture details. Send. Read what ChatGPT gives back. Edit the words until they sound like you, not like ChatGPT.

build.title: Save the words in a Google Doc
build.instructions: Title the doc WEBSITE COPY. Paste your three sections. Next session we put these words into Carrd.

test_output.title: Read your headline aloud
test_output.instruction: Read your headline aloud to the person next to you. They have never heard of your venture. Did they understand it? If not, rewrite the headline.

sessions.title: Build your simple website
sessions.set_the_stage:
Two hours. Your website goes live with a real URL anyone in the world can visit.

Carrd is the tool. The simplest website builder that exists. You pick a template, drop in your three sections of copy, add a big button that takes the visitor to your app, publish. Done.

Do not get pulled into colours and fonts for the first hour. Words first, layout next, styling at the end.

build.title: Sign up at carrd.co
build.instructions: Free account. Pick the simplest one-page template. Avoid templates with too many sections. You only need three.

build.title: Drop in your headline, then your three sections
build.instructions: Top: headline. Middle: who it is for. Middle-lower: how it works. Bottom: one big button that says VISIT THE APP and links to your Netlify URL from yesterday.

build.title: Open it on your phone
build.instructions: Your visitor will see it on a phone. Open your Carrd URL on your phone before you call it done. If the button is too small or the words too long, fix it now.

build.title: Publish at a custom URL
build.instructions: Free Carrd lets you publish at yourname.carrd.co. Pick something simple like rent-a-plant.carrd.co. This is the URL that goes on your portfolio.

output_capture.label: Your website URL
output_capture.instruction: Save the Carrd URL. Artifact 3 of 7.

sessions.title: Day 2 lunch
sessions.set_the_stage:
Lunch. The website is live. The app is live. Take a real break.

After lunch we make the audio and the video. The Minifounder who eats and rests does better afternoon work than the Minifounder who keeps polishing through the break.

build.title: Eat, walk, breathe
build.instructions: Be back at 14:00 sharp. Audio and video this afternoon.

sessions.title: Build your audio
sessions.set_the_stage:
One hour. Sixty seconds of audio that introduces your First Build in the visitor's ear.

Two steps. First, we write the script (with Gemini, NOT Claude — we save Claude). Then we generate the voice (with ElevenLabs free tier). Then we embed the audio on the website right under the headline.

When a visitor lands on your site, they should hear your venture explained in a real human-sounding voice within sixty seconds.

prompts.title: Audio script prompt for Gemini
prompts.content:
I am a Class 9 student. I built a small web app called [APP NAME] for [PERSON]. The app does [WHAT IT DOES, ONE LINE].

Write me a 60-second spoken script that introduces this. The script will be read aloud (by an AI voice). It should sound like a young person speaking, NOT a corporate ad. It should answer three things in sixty seconds:

1. What this is (one sentence)
2. Who it is for (one sentence)
3. One small example of how it helps them (two sentences)

Use plain English. Use short sentences. Add line breaks where the voice should pause.

build.title: Open Gemini in a new tab
build.instructions: Use gemini.google.com. Free. Paste the script prompt with your real details.

build.title: Edit the script to sound like you
build.instructions: Read the script aloud. If a sentence does not sound like something you would say, change it. The voice should sound like a real young person, not like a press release.

build.title: Generate the voice in ElevenLabs
build.instructions: Go to elevenlabs.io. Sign up free. Paste your script. Pick a voice that fits your venture. Click generate. Download as MP3.

build.title: Embed the audio on Carrd
build.instructions: In Carrd, add an Audio element right under the headline. Upload your MP3. Save and publish. Reload your live site on your phone to confirm it plays.

output_capture.label: Audio embedded on website
output_capture.instruction: Audio is live on the site. Artifact 4 of 7.

sessions.title: Build your intro video
sessions.set_the_stage:
One hour. Ninety seconds of video showing your First Build in action with your voice walking the visitor through it.

Loom is the tool. Free. You hit record, your screen and your face both get captured, you walk through your app like you are showing it to a friend, you hit stop, you get a URL.

This is the artifact your parents will probably watch first. Make it warm. Make it short.

prompts.title: Video script prompt for Gemini
prompts.content:
I am a Class 9 student. I built a small web app called [APP NAME] for [PERSON]. The app does [WHAT IT DOES].

Write me a 90-second script for a screen-recorded video where I will be talking over my screen. The video will show me clicking through the app while I narrate.

The script should have three parts:

1. INTRO (10 seconds): Who I am and what I built
2. WALKTHROUGH (60 seconds): I open the app, I show the catalog/main screen, I make a choice, I show the confirmation
3. CLOSE (20 seconds): Who this is really for and why I built it for them

Write it like spoken words, not written words. Short sentences. Casual. Include small notes for ME (like 'pause here' or 'click slowly') in brackets.

build.title: Get the script from Gemini
build.instructions: Same Gemini tab as the audio. Paste the video prompt with your details. Read the script aloud once before recording.

build.title: Sign up for Loom
build.instructions: Go to loom.com. Free account. Install the browser extension or use the web recorder.

build.title: Record once
build.instructions: Open your Carrd site in one tab and your app in another. Hit Loom record. Walk through your script. Hit stop. Watch it back once. If the first thirty seconds are clean, keep it. If you tripped, record again. Do not re-record more than three times.

build.title: Get the share URL
build.instructions: Loom gives you a share URL. Copy it. In Carrd, add a Video Embed element below the audio. Paste the Loom URL. Save and publish.

test_output.title: Show it to one person in the room
test_output.instruction: Pull up your live Carrd site on your phone. Hand the phone to someone. Let them play the audio and watch the video. Their face is the test. Smiling face means it works.

output_capture.label: Video embedded on website
output_capture.instruction: Video is live on the site. Artifact 5 of 7.

sessions.title: Interim demo
sessions.set_the_stage:
Twenty minutes. Stand up.

Every Minifounder gets sixty seconds. You open your live Carrd site on the big screen, you walk the room through one example, you sit down. The room claps. Next Minifounder.

This is not Demo Day. Demo Day is in two weeks with parents. This is just so the room sees what everyone made. Watch carefully. Every video and audio gives you ideas for the pitch deck you will build next week.

build.title: The order
build.instructions: Going around the room. Sixty seconds each. No questions. Save reactions for after.

build.title: While others demo, keep a list
build.instructions: Notes app on your phone. Write down anything you want to add to your pitch deck next week. The list is your homework.

sessions.title: What happens next
sessions.set_the_stage:
Last thirty minutes of the weekend.

You walked in yesterday morning. You leave with five live artifacts. Ideation sheet. App. Website. Audio. Video. Plus a brochure to make at home tonight or tomorrow as homework.

The next two weeks are not heads-down building like this weekend. Week 1 you build a pitch deck for your venture. Week 2 you stand on Demo Day with parents in the room.

build.title: Homework: build your brochure tonight or tomorrow
build.instructions: Open Canva. Free account. Pick a one-page brochure template. Title, one-line description of your venture, three points about who it helps and how, your Carrd URL at the bottom. Export as PDF. Link from your Carrd site. This is Artifact 6 of 7. Done at home, not in the workshop.

build.title: Week 1: the pitch deck
build.instructions: Saturday next week, 11:00 AM IST online. Ninety minutes office hours on Google Meet. You will come with a draft pitch deck (six slides) built using Gamma. The session is for review and coaching. We send the slide template on Monday.

build.title: Week 2: Demo Day
build.instructions: Weekend after that. In person. Four hours. Parents and teachers invited. Each Minifounder gets three minutes plus one minute of questions. Recorded. The recording goes on your portfolio for life.

build.title: Keep the WhatsApp group active
build.instructions: Stuck on the brochure? Stuck on Canva? Post in the group. Someone usually replies in an hour. The facilitator is in the group.

output_capture.label: Brochure (homework)
output_capture.instruction: Build at home in Canva. Linked from your Carrd. Artifact 6 of 7.

sessions.title: Week 1: Pitch deck review
sessions.set_the_stage:
Saturday morning. Online. Ninety minutes.

Every Minifounder walks into this session with a draft six-slide pitch deck built in Gamma. We sent the template on Monday. The deck tells the story of your venture in six slides: who you are, what problem you saw, who has it, what you built, how it works, what could happen next.

This session is review and coaching. Every Minifounder shares screen for five minutes. Three minutes to walk through the deck. Two minutes for the room to react. By the end of the call, you know exactly what to polish before Demo Day next weekend.

questions.title: Where does your deck stumble?
questions.body: Pick the one slide that does not feel right. Show that one first. The room helps you make it work.

questions.title: What surprised you while making it?
questions.body: Maybe the venture got smaller when you tried to explain it. Maybe it got bigger. Both are normal. Both teach you something for Demo Day.

prompts.title: Pitch deck prompt for Gamma
prompts.content:
Build me a 6-slide pitch deck for my school micro-venture. I am a Class 9 student. Here is my venture:

[PASTE YOUR IDEATION SHEET]

My app is live at: [YOUR CARRD URL]

Make the deck warm, simple, and visual. Not corporate. The six slides should be:

1. TITLE: My name, my venture name, one tagline
2. THE PROBLEM: Who has it, in one sentence, with one real example
3. WHO I AM BUILDING FOR: A short story about the real person
4. WHAT I BUILT: Screenshots/description of the app and website
5. HOW IT WORKS: Three steps the customer takes
6. WHAT COULD HAPPEN NEXT: If this worked for 20 customers, what would change?

Use a clean warm-coloured theme. Big visuals. Short text. The kind of deck a school student would actually present to their parents, not a startup investor.

build.title: Open Gamma if you have not yet
build.instructions: Go to gamma.app. Free tier gives you 400 credits. One deck takes 40 credits. Plenty of room.

build.title: Use the prompt with your details
build.instructions: Paste in your Ideation sheet and Carrd URL. Hit generate. Gamma builds the whole deck in a minute.

build.title: The round-robin
build.instructions: We go around. Each Minifounder shares screen, runs through their deck in three minutes. The room and facilitator give two minutes of reaction. Move on. Twelve Minifounders fit into 90 minutes.

build.title: Leave with one polish
build.instructions: By the end of the call, every Minifounder has one specific thing to fix on the deck before Demo Day. Not three. One. Specific.

output_capture.label: Your pitch deck
output_capture.instruction: Save the Gamma share URL. Artifact 7 of 7. The last artifact before Demo Day.

sessions.title: Demo Day
sessions.set_the_stage:
Saturday morning. In person. Parents in the chairs. Teachers along the side. Friends in the back.

Two weeks ago you walked into this room with nothing. Today every Minifounder has a portfolio page with seven artifacts. Today you stand up for three minutes and walk a room of people through what you made and who it is for.

We record every demo. The recording goes on your portfolio. It is the first thing you will show a college admissions person, an internship coordinator, or a future co-founder. Sometimes it is the first thing a relative shows their friends.

Four hours. Cup of chai in the middle. Mingling at the end.

build.title: Order
build.instructions: Alphabetical by first name. Three minutes plus one minute of questions. Hard cap. Facilitator keeps time.

build.title: The shape of three minutes
build.instructions: Minute 1: who you built for and why. Minute 2: live demo of your app on the big screen. Minute 3: what you learnt and one thing you would do next.

build.title: Recording
build.instructions: Camera on. Lapel mic for clarity. Talk to the front row, not the camera. The recording looks after itself.

build.title: Mingling and snacks
build.instructions: Thirty minutes after the last demo. Parents talk to Minifounders. Minifounders talk to each other. Photos. Then home.

build.title: Your portfolio is live
build.instructions: By Monday morning the Demo Day recording is added to your portfolio page. The URL goes to your parents by email. Your portfolio is now public and permanent.

output_capture.label: Demo Day recording
output_capture.instruction: Recording added to your portfolio by Monday. Plus your URL goes to your parents.

ideas.name: Rent-a-Plant
ideas.for_whom: Your mother who grows plants on the balcony, or a neighbour aunty who runs a small nursery
ideas.problem: Plants she has cannot find people who will look after them well. People who want plants do not want the commitment of buying.
ideas.what_app_does: Shows a catalog of available plants with photos and care notes. Lets customers rent a plant for three months. Confirms the rental. Shows updates on the plant over time.
ideas.what_makes_it_real: Customers exist (friends of family). Business model is real (₹200 per month per plant). No payment integration needed, money changes hands in person.

ideas.name: Tiffin Choice
ideas.for_whom: Your family or a neighbour aunty who runs a small tiffin service
ideas.problem: Customers do not know the weekly menu in advance. The aunty does not know who wants what until she has already cooked.
ideas.what_app_does: Shows the upcoming weekly menu. Lets regular customers pick their three preferred dishes for next week. Sends the preferences to the aunty.
ideas.what_makes_it_real: Real local business. Removes the daily WhatsApp guessing. The aunty cooks what people actually want. Customers pay in person as always.

ideas.name: Rent-a-Tree
ideas.for_whom: Anyone in your extended family who has a fruit tree in their backyard or village
ideas.problem: Fruit trees produce more than the family can eat. The fruit rots. Strangers cannot easily buy from a single household.
ideas.what_app_does: Shows the trees available, the fruit each one produces, and when. Lets a customer subscribe to a tree for a season. Shows updates on flowering, ripening, and harvest.
ideas.what_makes_it_real: Real fruit, real season, real customers. Borrowed directly from the orchard rental model. Works for one mango tree in a grandparent's backyard.

ideas.name: Birthday Box
ideas.for_whom: Yourself or your friend whose family makes return-gift boxes for kids' birthday parties
ideas.problem: Parents organising kids' birthdays do not know what to put in the return-gift bags. The bag-maker has good ideas but no way to show them.
ideas.what_app_does: Shows three themed boxes (Unicorn, Dinosaur, Space) at three price points. Parent picks theme, quantity, and party date. Submits the order. Confirmation page tells them what happens next.
ideas.what_makes_it_real: Real product, real makers, real customers. Built around a real seasonal business cycle. Birthday season is year-round.

ideas.name: Tutoring Slots
ideas.for_whom: Your elder sister, brother, or cousin who tutors younger students (Class 5–7) on weekday evenings
ideas.problem: Parents trying to book a tutor have to make many phone calls. The tutor wastes evenings confirming and re-confirming slots over WhatsApp.
ideas.what_app_does: Shows the tutor's subjects, sample lesson topics, available time slots, and recent students' progress. Parents pick a free trial slot. Booking is confirmed in person.
ideas.what_makes_it_real: Real tutor, real students, real recurring revenue. Built around a real micro-venture inside the student's family or social circle.
